questions.question: How do I track my package?
questions.top_title: Where is my package? Tracking Status Help
questions.answer:
To track your USPS package, you can check the latest tracking status by visiting the USPS tracking page at: https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input

You'll need your tracking number to get the current status and location of your package.

I also recommend signing up for **Informed Delivery®** notifications, which offers a digital preview of your mail and allows you to manage your expected packages. You can sign up at: https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm

The tracking system shows various status updates as your package moves through the postal network, from when the shipping label is created all the way through delivery.

**Source:** Where is my package? Tracking Status Help — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the retrieved context, accurately citing the tracking URL and Informed Delivery service without adding any fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to track a package by providing the specific USPS tracking link and recommending the Informed Delivery service.

overall.reason: The answer is accurate, relevant, and provides actionable information with proper source citation to help the user track their package.

questions.question: What do I do if my package is missing?
questions.top_title: Where is my package? Tracking Status Help
questions.answer:
If your package shows as "Delivered" but you can't find it, here's what you should do:

**First, check these locations:**
- Garage
- With others in your household
- Mailbox (packages may come separately from regular mail; check again later)
- Porch
- Areas protected from weather
- Areas out of street view, such as under a mat or near a back entrance
- All exterior doors and any locations where the package could be placed

**For multi-unit buildings:**
- If you have a community mailbox with a key inside, your package is in a parcel locker - check the key tag for the locker number
- If you live in an apartment/condo, contact your building's management office

**If you still can't find your package:**
Wait at least one postal business day after the 'delivered' scan, then visit the "email us" page on usps.com to submit a service request to your local Post Office.

For other missing mail situations, you can also:
- Submit a Missing Mail search request
- File a domestic claim if appropriate
- Contact customer service at 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET)

**Source:** Where is my package? Tracking Status Help — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package

faithfulness.reason: The answer is fully grounded in the provided context with no hallucinations or fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses what to do if a package is missing with comprehensive, actionable steps.

overall.reason: The response provides a well-organized, complete answer that accurately reflects the source material and fully addresses the user's question.

questions.question: How do I hold my mail while on vacation?
questions.answer:
To hold your mail while on vacation, you can use USPS Hold Mail® service, which will hold **ALL** mail (including letters and packages) for **ALL** individuals at your address. Here are your options:

**Online (Recommended):**
- Visit [holdmail.usps.com/holdmail](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/)
- You'll need to sign in to or create a USPS.com account
- You can submit requests up to 30 days in advance
- Requests submitted before 2:00 AM Central Time can start the same postal business day
- Requests submitted after 2:00 AM Central Time start the next postal business day

**In Person:**
- Visit your local Post Office and complete PS Form 8076 (Authorization to Hold Mail)
- Use the [Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) to find your nearest location

**By Phone:**
- Call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

**Important notes:**
- The service is **free**
- Only one Hold Mail request can be active per address at a time
- Save your confirmation number for future reference
- The service holds mail for all residents at the address, not just individual people

Source: USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the retrieved context with no fabricated information or hallucinations.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses the question about holding mail during vacation by providing comprehensive instructions and options for using USPS Hold Mail service.

overall.reason: The answer is excellent, providing accurate, well-organized, and complete information that fully addresses the user's vacation mail holding needs.

questions.question: How do I change my address?
questions.answer:
You can change your address with USPS in two ways: **online or in person**.

**Online (Fastest Option):**
Visit https://moversguide.usps.com/ to submit your change of address request. You'll need:
- A mobile phone number for verification (you'll receive a one-time passcode)
- A valid credit or debit card ($1.25 authentication fee) - the billing address must match either your old or new address
- A valid email address for communications

Note: Prepaid cards and gift cards are not accepted.

**In Person:**
Visit any of the 33,000+ local Post Office locations to complete your request.

**Important Timing:**
- Submit your request up to 90 days **before** your move date
- Or up to 30 days **after** your move date
- For best results, notify everyone who sends you mail at least 2 weeks before moving

**Pro Tip:** Make the process easier by directly notifying your banks, utilities, and other mail senders of your new address change in addition to filing with USPS.

If online authentication fails, you'll receive email instructions to complete identity verification at a Post Office with photo ID.

**Source:** Change of Address - The Basics — https://moversguide.usps.com/mgo/disclaimer

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no hallucinations or fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly and comprehensively addresses how to change your address with clear step-by-step instructions.

overall.reason: The answer is well-structured, accurate, complete, and provides all essential information from the context in a user-friendly format.

questions.question: How do I get a PO Box?
questions.answer:

questions.question: How do I request a redelivery?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, here's how you can request a redelivery:

**To schedule a Redelivery:**
You can schedule a Redelivery online using either:
- A tracking number, OR
- The barcode number shown on the back of your PS Form 3849 ("We ReDeliver for You!" notice)

**What is Redelivery:**
If USPS missed you during delivery, you'll receive a PS Form 3849 notice. This happens when:
- Your mailbox was full
- No parcel lockers were available
- The item was too large for your mail receptacle
- A signature or payment was required
- The item couldn't be left in a secure place

**Cost:**
Scheduling a Redelivery is free.

**Note:** The context mentions that you can get started with Redelivery and check service availability in your area by entering your contact information, but the specific website or steps aren't fully detailed in this excerpt.

If you need more specific instructions on where exactly to go online to schedule your redelivery, I'd recommend calling 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visiting usps.com/help/contact-us.htm for complete details.

**Source:** Redelivery - The Basics — (URL not provided in context)

questions.question: How do I sign up for Informed Delivery?
questions.answer:
To sign up for Informed Delivery, visit **informeddelivery.usps.com** to check if your address is eligible and complete the sign-up process.

**Important eligibility note:** While Informed Delivery is available to most residential, business, and eligible PO Box addresses, there are some exceptions. In rare cases, even if you live in an eligible ZIP Code, you may not be able to sign up if your individual mailbox is not uniquely coded in USPS systems. This coding ensures users can only view mail destined for their address. Addresses in high-density areas like apartment buildings or condos are more likely to have this limitation.

The sign-up website will let you know immediately if your specific address is eligible for the service.

**Source: Informed Delivery® - The Basics — https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action**

questions.question: How do I print a shipping label online?
questions.top_title: How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label?
questions.answer:
To print a shipping label online using USPS Click-N-Ship®, follow these 4 easy steps:

1. **Enter Label Information**: Fill in the return and delivery addresses, package details, optional package value, and shipping date. Note that domestic labels must include a domestic return address - foreign return addresses are not allowed on domestic mail and packages.

2. **Select Service**: Choose your desired shipping service and any extra services by clicking the corresponding radio button, then click "Add to Cart & Start New Label" to create another label or "Add to Cart" to review your shipping cart.

3. **Review and Pay**: Review your label information on the Shipping Cart page. Edit any incorrect information if needed, then click "Next: Billing Information" to purchase your labels. Select your payment method, enter the required information, and click "Next: Pay and Print."

4. **Print Your Label**: From the Payment Confirmation page, you can print your label(s) immediately, save them as a PDF file for later printing, create another label, or schedule a pickup. Labels can also be printed from your Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date.

**No printer available?** You can use the Label Broker® feature - pay for your shipping label online and select "Print Labels Later at the Post Office" option. You'll receive a QR code via email to take to any Post Office location with your package, where they can scan the code and print your label.

**Source**: How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label? — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label

faithfulness.reason: The answer is entirely based on the retrieved context with no fabricated information or hallucinations.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to print shipping labels online with clear step-by-step instructions.

overall.reason: Excellent response that accurately summarizes the context into a well-structured, comprehensive guide for printing shipping labels online.

questions.question: Can I ship lithium batteries?
questions.answer:
Yes, you can ship lithium batteries, but there are specific restrictions depending on whether you're shipping domestically or internationally, and how the batteries are packaged.

**Domestic Shipments (including Alaska and Hawaii):**
- **Batteries installed in equipment**: Mailable by both surface and air transportation
- **Batteries packed with equipment (not installed)**: Mailable by both surface and air transportation  
- **Individual batteries in original sealed packaging**: Mailable by surface transportation only, but prohibited by air transportation (except limited quantities intra-Alaska)
- **Damaged, defective, or recalled batteries**: Prohibited unless approved by the director, Product Classification

**International Shipments:**
- **Only NEW batteries properly installed in electronic devices** are mailable to many international destinations and military addresses (APO/FPO/DPO)
- **Prohibited internationally**: New batteries packed with (but not installed in) equipment, individual batteries shipped separately, and any used, damaged, defective, or recalled batteries

Different countries may have additional specific requirements, so check the Postal Explorer or Individual Country Listings for destination-specific restrictions.

Source: Can I Ship Lithium Batteries? — https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm#lithium-batteries

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no fabricated information or hallucinations.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses the question about shipping lithium batteries with comprehensive coverage of domestic and international shipping rules.

overall.reason: The answer is excellent, providing accurate, well-organized, and complete information that fully satisfies the user's question.

questions.question: How do I mail cremated remains?
questions.answer:
To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these specific requirements:

**Required Service:**
- Cremated remains must be shipped using **Priority Mail Express** only (or Priority Mail Express International for international shipments). No other shipping service is permitted.

**Required Packaging:**
- You must use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE). This special box is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or other artisan works.

**How to Get Supplies:**
- Cremated Remains kits can be ordered **free of charge** from the Postal Store at USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- You can also order packaging through Click-N-Ship when creating a shipping label
- Some local post offices may have kits available behind the counter, but availability varies

**Shipping Guidelines:**
- Follow the guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package
- Priority Mail Express comes with USPS Tracking

**International Shipping:**
- Cremated remains can be shipped internationally only when the destination country permits such contents and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country

**Important Note:** Starting March 1, 2025, customers are required to use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box for all shipments.

Source: Shipping Cremated Remains and Ashes — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes

questions.question: What are the size restrictions for mailing a package?
questions.answer:
Based on USPS guidelines, here are the key size restrictions for mailing packages:

**Maximum Size Limits:**
- **Standard parcels**: Cannot exceed 108 inches in length and girth combined
- **USPS Ground Advantage**: Cannot exceed 130 inches in length and girth combined (this is an exception to the standard limit)

**Important Notes:**
- USPS Ground Advantage items measuring between 108-130 inches will be charged at the oversized price
- Lower maximum size standards may apply to certain destinations (the context mentions this but doesn't specify details)

**How Size is Measured:**
- **Length**: Always the longest dimension (in inches)
- **Girth**: Distance around the thickest part of the package
  - For rectangular packages: (Height × 2) + (Width × 2)
  - For cylindrical packages: The circumference of the cylinder

Large, lightweight packages may be subject to special pricing like balloon price, oversized price, or dimensional weight pricing depending on the mail class.

For specific pricing or if your package exceeds these dimensions, I'd recommend using the USPS Postage Price Calculator or contacting USPS directly.

**Source:** Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards

faithfulness.reason: The answer is fully grounded in the provided context with accurate information about size limits, measurements, and pricing exceptions.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses the question about package size restrictions with clear, specific information about maximum dimensions and measurement methods.

questions.question: How do I file a claim for a damaged package?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, filing a claim for a damaged international package is a two-step process:

**Step 1: File an International Inquiry First**
Before you can file a claim, you must first complete an international inquiry. This is required to investigate whether your item was delivered or received in good condition.

**Step 2: File the Claim**
If the inquiry results determine your item was damaged, your case will then be sent to the claim process. A claim is a request for payment due to damage to insured items.

**To Get Started:**
Visit [File an International Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) to begin the international inquiry process.

**Important Notes:**
- The international inquiry must be filed within a certain time frame from the country of origin
- Your next steps will depend on whether the item was shipped to or from the United States, the mail class, and what happened to the item
- You can check your item's mail class by entering the tracking number into USPS Tracking®

However, the provided context only covers international packages. If your damaged package was domestic (within the US), I don't have that information available. For domestic claims or more detailed guidance, please call 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visit usps.com/help/contact-us.htm.

Source: International Inquiries - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the retrieved context, accurately representing the two-step process and all specific details provided.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses filing claims for damaged packages but is limited to international packages only, which may not fully satisfy the general question.

overall.reason: Well-structured and accurate answer that acknowledges its limitations regarding domestic packages and provides helpful next steps.

questions.question: How do I send certified mail?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, here's how to send Certified Mail:

**To send Certified Mail:**

1. **Purchase at a Post Office** - Certified Mail can only be purchased at the time of mailing at a Post Office location

2. **Complete the form** - Fill out PS Form 3800 (Receipt for Certified Mail) with the complete name and address of the person or firm you're sending to

3. **Choose your mail class** - Certified Mail can be added to eligible mail classes and travels at the speed of whatever mail class you select

4. **Optional add-ons** - You can combine Certified Mail with other services like:
   - Return Receipt Service (for proof of signature)
   - Restricted Delivery (delivery only to addressee)
   - Adult Signature Required

**What you'll receive:**
- A mailing receipt (Form 3800) as confirmation the item was sent
- A tracking number to check delivery status online at usps.com, by phone, or through electronic file transfer

The recipient will need to sign for the item upon delivery.

**Source: Certified Mail® - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certified-Mail-The-Basics**

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to send certified mail with clear, actionable steps.

overall.reason: The answer is comprehensive, well-organized, and provides all essential information needed to send certified mail.

questions.question: What is USPS Media Mail and what can I ship with it?
questions.answer:
Media Mail® is an economy shipping solution offered by USPS for sending media items. Here's what you can ship with Media Mail:

**Eligible Items:**
- Books
- Video and sound recordings
- Printed music
- Recorded computer-readable media (such as CDs and DVDs)
- Film and manuscripts
- Computer diskettes

**Key Features:**
- Packages can weigh up to 70 lbs
- Maximum size: 108 inches in combined length and distance around the thickest part
- Economy pricing for qualified media items

**Important Restrictions:**
- Media Mail cannot contain advertising (books may include incidental book announcements)
- Video games, computer drives, and digital drives do NOT qualify
- Including non-qualifying items can result in higher postage rates
- Contents may be inspected by the Postal Service to verify eligibility

**Note:** Media Mail is not sealed against postal inspection, so the Postal Service can check contents to ensure proper pricing.

For complete requirements and pricing information, you can visit the links provided or check the Domestic Mail Manual.

Source: What is Media Mail®? — https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm#mediamail

questions.question: How do I request a redelivery?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, here's how you can request a redelivery:

**To schedule a Redelivery:**
You can schedule a Redelivery online using either:
- A tracking number, OR
- The barcode number shown on the back of your PS Form 3849 ("We ReDeliver for You!" notice)

**What is Redelivery:**
If USPS missed you during delivery, you'll receive a PS Form 3849 notice. This happens when:
- Your mailbox was full
- No parcel lockers were available
- The item was too large for your mail receptacle
- A signature or payment was required
- The item couldn't be left in a secure place

**Cost:**
Scheduling a Redelivery is free.

**Note:** The context mentions that you can get started with Redelivery and check service availability in your area by entering your contact information, but the specific website or steps aren't fully detailed in this excerpt.

If you need more specific instructions on where exactly to go online to schedule your redelivery, I'd recommend calling 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visiting usps.com/help/contact-us.htm for complete details.

**Source:** Redelivery - The Basics — (URL not provided in context)

questions.question: How do I sign up for Informed Delivery?
questions.answer:
To sign up for Informed Delivery, visit **informeddelivery.usps.com** to check if your address is eligible and complete the sign-up process.

**Important eligibility note:** While Informed Delivery is available to most residential, business, and eligible PO Box addresses, there are some exceptions. In rare cases, even if you live in an eligible ZIP Code, you may not be able to sign up if your individual mailbox is not uniquely coded in USPS systems. This coding ensures users can only view mail destined for their address. Addresses in high-density areas like apartment buildings or condos are more likely to have this limitation.

The sign-up website will let you know immediately if your specific address is eligible for the service.

**Source: Informed Delivery® - The Basics — https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action**

questions.question: How do I print a shipping label online?
questions.top_title: How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label?
questions.answer:
To print a shipping label online using USPS Click-N-Ship®, follow these 4 easy steps:

1. **Enter Label Information**: Fill in the return and delivery addresses, package details, optional package value, and shipping date. Note that domestic labels must include a domestic return address - foreign return addresses are not allowed on domestic mail and packages.

2. **Select Service**: Choose your desired shipping service and any extra services by clicking the corresponding radio button, then click "Add to Cart & Start New Label" to create another label or "Add to Cart" to review your shipping cart.

3. **Review and Pay**: Review your label information on the Shipping Cart page. Edit any incorrect information if needed, then click "Next: Billing Information" to purchase your labels. Select your payment method, enter the required information, and click "Next: Pay and Print."

4. **Print Your Label**: From the Payment Confirmation page, you can print your label(s) immediately, save them as a PDF file for later printing, create another label, or schedule a pickup. Labels can also be printed from your Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date.

**No printer available?** You can use the Label Broker® feature - pay for your shipping label online and select "Print Labels Later at the Post Office" option. You'll receive a QR code via email to take to any Post Office location with your package, where they can scan the code and print your label.

**Source**: How do I print a Click-N-Ship® label? — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/How-do-I-print-a-Click-N-Ship-label

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to print shipping labels online with clear step-by-step instructions.

overall.reason: Excellent response that accurately summarizes the context into a well-structured, comprehensive guide for printing shipping labels online.

questions.question: Can I ship lithium batteries?
questions.answer:
Yes, you can ship lithium batteries, but there are specific restrictions depending on whether you're shipping domestically or internationally, and how the batteries are packaged.

**Domestic Shipments (including Alaska and Hawaii):**
- **Batteries installed in equipment**: Mailable by both surface and air transportation
- **Batteries packed with equipment (not installed)**: Mailable by both surface and air transportation  
- **Individual batteries in original sealed packaging**: Mailable by surface transportation only, but prohibited by air transportation (except limited quantities intra-Alaska)
- **Damaged, defective, or recalled batteries**: Prohibited unless approved by the director, Product Classification

**International Shipments:**
- **Only NEW batteries properly installed in electronic devices** are mailable to many international destinations and military addresses (APO/FPO/DPO)
- **Prohibited internationally**: New batteries packed with (but not installed in) equipment, individual batteries shipped separately, and any used, damaged, defective, or recalled batteries

Different countries may have additional specific requirements, so check the Postal Explorer or Individual Country Listings for destination-specific restrictions.

Source: Can I Ship Lithium Batteries? — https://www.usps.com/ship/shipping-restrictions.htm#lithium-batteries

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no fabricated information or hallucinations.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses the question about shipping lithium batteries with comprehensive coverage of domestic and international shipping rules.

overall.reason: The answer is excellent, providing accurate, well-organized, and complete information that fully satisfies the user's question.

questions.question: How do I mail cremated remains?
questions.answer:
To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these specific requirements:

**Required Service:**
- Cremated remains must be shipped using **Priority Mail Express** only (or Priority Mail Express International for international shipments). No other shipping service is permitted.

**Required Packaging:**
- You must use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE). This special box is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or other artisan works.

**How to Get Supplies:**
- Cremated Remains kits can be ordered **free of charge** from the Postal Store at USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- You can also order packaging through Click-N-Ship when creating a shipping label
- Some local post offices may have kits available behind the counter, but availability varies

**Shipping Guidelines:**
- Follow the guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package
- Priority Mail Express comes with USPS Tracking

**International Shipping:**
- Cremated remains can be shipped internationally only when the destination country permits such contents and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country

**Important Note:** Starting March 1, 2025, customers are required to use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box for all shipments.

Source: Shipping Cremated Remains and Ashes — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes

questions.question: What are the size restrictions for mailing a package?
questions.answer:
Based on USPS guidelines, here are the key size restrictions for mailing packages:

**Maximum Size Limits:**
- **Standard parcels**: Cannot exceed 108 inches in length and girth combined
- **USPS Ground Advantage**: Cannot exceed 130 inches in length and girth combined (this is an exception to the standard limit)

**Important Notes:**
- USPS Ground Advantage items measuring between 108-130 inches will be charged at the oversized price
- Lower maximum size standards may apply to certain destinations (the context mentions this but doesn't specify details)

**How Size is Measured:**
- **Length**: Always the longest dimension (in inches)
- **Girth**: Distance around the thickest part of the package
  - For rectangular packages: (Height × 2) + (Width × 2)
  - For cylindrical packages: The circumference of the cylinder

Large, lightweight packages may be subject to special pricing like balloon price, oversized price, or dimensional weight pricing depending on the mail class.

For specific pricing or if your package exceeds these dimensions, I'd recommend using the USPS Postage Price Calculator or contacting USPS directly.

**Source:** Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards

questions.question: How do I file a claim for a damaged package?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, filing a claim for a damaged international package is a two-step process:

**Step 1: File an International Inquiry First**
Before you can file a claim, you must first complete an international inquiry. This is required to investigate whether your item was delivered or received in good condition.

**Step 2: File the Claim**
If the inquiry results determine your item was damaged, your case will then be sent to the claim process. A claim is a request for payment due to damage to insured items.

**To Get Started:**
Visit [File an International Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) to begin the international inquiry process.

**Important Notes:**
- The international inquiry must be filed within a certain time frame from the country of origin
- Your next steps will depend on whether the item was shipped to or from the United States, the mail class, and what happened to the item
- You can check your item's mail class by entering the tracking number into USPS Tracking®

However, the provided context only covers international packages. If your damaged package was domestic (within the US), I don't have that information available. For domestic claims or more detailed guidance, please call 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visit usps.com/help/contact-us.htm.

Source: International Inquiries - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the retrieved context, accurately representing the two-step process and all specific details provided.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses filing claims for damaged packages but is limited to international packages only, which may not fully satisfy the general question.

overall.reason: Well-structured and accurate answer that acknowledges its limitations regarding domestic packages and provides helpful next steps.

questions.question: How do I send certified mail?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, here's how to send Certified Mail:

**To send Certified Mail:**

1. **Purchase at a Post Office** - Certified Mail can only be purchased at the time of mailing at a Post Office location

2. **Complete the form** - Fill out PS Form 3800 (Receipt for Certified Mail) with the complete name and address of the person or firm you're sending to

3. **Choose your mail class** - Certified Mail can be added to eligible mail classes and travels at the speed of whatever mail class you select

4. **Optional add-ons** - You can combine Certified Mail with other services like:
   - Return Receipt Service (for proof of signature)
   - Restricted Delivery (delivery only to addressee)
   - Adult Signature Required

**What you'll receive:**
- A mailing receipt (Form 3800) as confirmation the item was sent
- A tracking number to check delivery status online at usps.com, by phone, or through electronic file transfer

The recipient will need to sign for the item upon delivery.

**Source: Certified Mail® - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certified-Mail-The-Basics**

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to send certified mail with clear, actionable steps.

overall.reason: The answer is comprehensive, well-organized, and provides all essential information needed to send certified mail.

questions.question: What is USPS Media Mail and what can I ship with it?
questions.answer:
Media Mail® is an economy shipping solution offered by USPS for sending media items. Here's what you can ship with Media Mail:

**Eligible Items:**
- Books
- Video and sound recordings
- Printed music
- Recorded computer-readable media (such as CDs and DVDs)
- Film and manuscripts
- Computer diskettes

**Key Features:**
- Packages can weigh up to 70 lbs
- Maximum size: 108 inches in combined length and distance around the thickest part
- Economy pricing for qualified media items

**Important Restrictions:**
- Media Mail cannot contain advertising (books may include incidental book announcements)
- Video games, computer drives, and digital drives do NOT qualify
- Including non-qualifying items can result in higher postage rates
- Contents may be inspected by the Postal Service to verify eligibility

**Note:** Media Mail is not sealed against postal inspection, so the Postal Service can check contents to ensure proper pricing.

For complete requirements and pricing information, you can visit the links provided or check the Domestic Mail Manual.

Source: What is Media Mail®? — https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm#mediamail

questions.question: How do I track my package?
questions.top_title: Where is my package? Tracking Status Help
questions.answer:
To track your USPS package, you can check the latest tracking status by visiting the USPS tracking page at: https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input

You'll need your tracking number to get the current status and location of your package.

I also recommend signing up for **Informed Delivery®** notifications, which offers a digital preview of your mail and allows you to manage your expected packages. You can sign up at: https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm

The tracking system shows various status updates as your package moves through the postal network, from when the shipping label is created all the way through delivery.

**Source:** Where is my package? Tracking Status Help — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Where-is-my-package

questions.question: How do I hold my mail while on vacation?
questions.answer:
To hold your mail while on vacation, you can use USPS Hold Mail® service, which will hold **ALL** mail (including letters and packages) for **ALL** individuals at your address. Here are your options:

**Online (Recommended):**
- Visit [holdmail.usps.com/holdmail](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/)
- You'll need to sign in to or create a USPS.com account
- You can submit requests up to 30 days in advance
- Requests submitted before 2:00 AM Central Time can start the same postal business day
- Requests submitted after 2:00 AM Central Time start the next postal business day

**In Person:**
- Visit your local Post Office and complete PS Form 8076 (Authorization to Hold Mail)
- Use the [Post Office Locator](https://tools.usps.com/find-location.htm) to find your nearest location

**By Phone:**
- Call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

**Important notes:**
- The service is **free**
- Only one Hold Mail request can be active per address at a time
- Save your confirmation number for future reference
- The service holds mail for all residents at the address, not just individual people

Source: USPS Hold Mail® - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/USPS-Hold-Mail-The-Basics

questions.question: How do I change my address?
questions.answer:
You can change your address with USPS in two ways: **online or in person**.

**Online (Fastest Option):**
Visit https://moversguide.usps.com/ to submit your change of address request. You'll need:
- A mobile phone number for verification (you'll receive a one-time passcode)
- A valid credit or debit card ($1.25 authentication fee) - the billing address must match either your old or new address
- A valid email address for communications

Note: Prepaid cards and gift cards are not accepted.

**In Person:**
Visit any of the 33,000+ local Post Office locations to complete your request.

**Important Timing:**
- Submit your request up to 90 days **before** your move date
- Or up to 30 days **after** your move date
- For best results, notify everyone who sends you mail at least 2 weeks before moving

**Pro Tip:** Make the process easier by directly notifying your banks, utilities, and other mail senders of your new address change in addition to filing with USPS.

If online authentication fails, you'll receive email instructions to complete identity verification at a Post Office with photo ID.

**Source:** Change of Address - The Basics — https://moversguide.usps.com/mgo/disclaimer

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly and comprehensively addresses how to change your address with clear step-by-step instructions.

questions.question: How do I mail cremated remains?
questions.answer:
To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these specific requirements:

**Required Service:**
- Cremated remains must be shipped using **Priority Mail Express** only (or Priority Mail Express International for international shipments). No other shipping service is permitted.

**Required Packaging:**
- You must use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE). This special box is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or other artisan works.

**How to Get Supplies:**
- Cremated Remains kits can be ordered **free of charge** from the Postal Store at USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- You can also order packaging through Click-N-Ship when creating a shipping label
- Some local post offices may have kits available behind the counter, but availability varies

**Shipping Guidelines:**
- Follow the guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package
- Priority Mail Express comes with USPS Tracking

**International Shipping:**
- Cremated remains can be shipped internationally only when the destination country permits such contents and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country

**Important Note:** Starting March 1, 2025, customers are required to use the USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box for all shipments.

Source: Shipping Cremated Remains and Ashes — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Shipping-Cremated-Remains-and-Ashes

questions.question: How do I send a money order?
questions.answer:
Based on the retrieved context, I can provide information about purchasing money orders, but the specific steps for sending a money order are not detailed in this content.

Here's what I can tell you about getting a money order:

**Where to purchase:**
- Any Post Office™ location
- From rural carriers and some contract stations (contact to confirm availability)
- Military money orders must be purchased at APO/FPO/DPO facilities

**Purchase limits:**
- Maximum $1,000 per domestic money order
- Multiple money orders can be purchased in one visit
- If you buy $3,000+ in money orders in one day, you'll need to complete Form 8105-A and show acceptable ID

**Payment methods accepted:**
- U.S. currency and coins
- ATM/Debit cards (with PIN entry)
- Checks, credit cards, and traveler's checks are NOT accepted

However, the retrieved content doesn't include the specific process for addressing, filling out, or mailing a money order. For complete instructions on how to send a money order, I recommend calling 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visiting usps.com/help/contact-us.htm.

Source: Money Orders - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Money-Orders-The-Basics

questions.question: What are the size restrictions for mailing a package?
questions.answer:
Based on USPS guidelines, here are the key size restrictions for mailing packages:

**Maximum Size Limits:**
- **Standard parcels**: Cannot exceed 108 inches in length and girth combined
- **USPS Ground Advantage**: Cannot exceed 130 inches in length and girth combined (this is an exception to the standard limit)

**Important Notes:**
- USPS Ground Advantage items measuring between 108-130 inches will be charged at the oversized price
- Lower maximum size standards may apply to certain destinations (the context mentions this but doesn't specify details)

**How Size is Measured:**
- **Length**: Always the longest dimension (in inches)
- **Girth**: Distance around the thickest part of the package
  - For rectangular packages: (Height × 2) + (Width × 2)
  - For cylindrical packages: The circumference of the cylinder

Large, lightweight packages may be subject to special pricing like balloon price, oversized price, or dimensional weight pricing depending on the mail class.

For specific pricing or if your package exceeds these dimensions, I'd recommend using the USPS Postage Price Calculator or contacting USPS directly.

**Source:** Parcel Size, Weight & Fee Standards — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Parcel-Size-Weight-Fee-Standards

questions.question: How do I file a claim for a damaged package?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, filing a claim for a damaged international package is a two-step process:

**Step 1: File an International Inquiry First**
Before you can file a claim, you must first complete an international inquiry. This is required to investigate whether your item was delivered or received in good condition.

**Step 2: File the Claim**
If the inquiry results determine your item was damaged, your case will then be sent to the claim process. A claim is a request for payment due to damage to insured items.

**To Get Started:**
Visit [File an International Claim](https://www.usps.com/help/international-claims.htm) to begin the international inquiry process.

**Important Notes:**
- The international inquiry must be filed within a certain time frame from the country of origin
- Your next steps will depend on whether the item was shipped to or from the United States, the mail class, and what happened to the item
- You can check your item's mail class by entering the tracking number into USPS Tracking®

However, the provided context only covers international packages. If your damaged package was domestic (within the US), I don't have that information available. For domestic claims or more detailed guidance, please call 1-800-275-8777 (Mon–Fri 8 AM–8:30 PM ET, Sat 8 AM–6 PM ET) or visit usps.com/help/contact-us.htm.

Source: International Inquiries - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/International-Inquiries-The-Basics

questions.question: How do I send certified mail?
questions.answer:
Based on the information provided, here's how to send Certified Mail:

**To send Certified Mail:**

1. **Purchase at a Post Office** - Certified Mail can only be purchased at the time of mailing at a Post Office location

2. **Complete the form** - Fill out PS Form 3800 (Receipt for Certified Mail) with the complete name and address of the person or firm you're sending to

3. **Choose your mail class** - Certified Mail can be added to eligible mail classes and travels at the speed of whatever mail class you select

4. **Optional add-ons** - You can combine Certified Mail with other services like:
   - Return Receipt Service (for proof of signature)
   - Restricted Delivery (delivery only to addressee)
   - Adult Signature Required

**What you'll receive:**
- A mailing receipt (Form 3800) as confirmation the item was sent
- A tracking number to check delivery status online at usps.com, by phone, or through electronic file transfer

The recipient will need to sign for the item upon delivery.

**Source: Certified Mail® - The Basics — https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Certified-Mail-The-Basics**

faithfulness.reason: The answer is completely grounded in the provided context with no fabricated information.

answer_relevance.reason: The answer directly addresses how to send certified mail with clear, actionable steps.

overall.reason: The answer is comprehensive, well-organized, and provides all essential information needed to send certified mail.

questions.question: What is USPS Media Mail and what can I ship with it?
questions.answer:
Media Mail® is an economy shipping solution offered by USPS for sending media items. Here's what you can ship with Media Mail:

**Eligible Items:**
- Books
- Video and sound recordings
- Printed music
- Recorded computer-readable media (such as CDs and DVDs)
- Film and manuscripts
- Computer diskettes

**Key Features:**
- Packages can weigh up to 70 lbs
- Maximum size: 108 inches in combined length and distance around the thickest part
- Economy pricing for qualified media items

**Important Restrictions:**
- Media Mail cannot contain advertising (books may include incidental book announcements)
- Video games, computer drives, and digital drives do NOT qualify
- Including non-qualifying items can result in higher postage rates
- Contents may be inspected by the Postal Service to verify eligibility

**Note:** Media Mail is not sealed against postal inspection, so the Postal Service can check contents to ensure proper pricing.

For complete requirements and pricing information, you can visit the links provided or check the Domestic Mail Manual.

Source: What is Media Mail®? — https://www.usps.com/ship/mail-shipping-services.htm#mediamail
